sessions.summary: Initial project session — OCR extraction, story graph, path enumeration, SVG visualization
sessions.note: Structured summary based on AI-Instructions.md and Codebase.md. Raw original log is at ../AI-session-log-20260408950.json.

message.text: Write a script to extract the text of 'the-cave-of-time'. Some pages have 'if you do X go to page 4 if not go to page 5' type continuations, so each possible starting point should be the beginning of a new text file. The text files should be named 00-CoT.txt where the first 2 digits correspond to the page number. You will also need to create a graph of all the possible paths the story can proceed since it has branching story lines. Save the graph of story possibilities in a separate file.

response.summary: Created initial extraction script and graph builder. Established NN-CoT.txt naming convention.

message.text: Run the script and create the text files

response.summary: Ran extraction script, generated initial cot-pages/ directory with text files.

message.text: Write a script to write all possible stories. Save them into a separate directory

response.summary: Created write_all_stories.py, generated story files into a stories directory.

message.text: Rerun it from a single canonical start page (for example page 10) to produce a smaller, stricter story set

response.summary: Reran story generation from page 10 as canonical start.

message.text: The previous command was taking too long, so I stopped it. Was it in an infinite loop? Make sure it does not happen again. Write out all the possible stories starting from the single page. If a story can go on infinitely, or if you have already gone more than 20 decision points, end the story there. All the stories should be starting from 'You've hiked through Snake Canyon once before while visiting your Uncle Howard ...' which is on page-2 of the PDF. Check the extracted COT pages to make sure that page-2 and other pages have been extracted properly.

response.summary: Added cycle detection and max 20 decision-point safeguard to write_all_stories.py. Identified OCR extraction starting from wrong PDF page.

message.text: OCR is not working properly. Page-2 of the story is in fact page-8 of the PDF. Each PDF page has two pages (spread scan). Page-8 of the PDF has story pages 2 and 3. Page-9 has story pages 4 and 5. Provided verified text for pages 2-5 with correct beginnings and endings. Extract text more carefully, confirm before proceeding.

response.summary: Updated reextract_cot_ocr_split.py to handle spread layout — splits each PDF page into left/right halves, maps PDF pages to story pages correctly (PDF pg 8 = story pgs 2-3). Re-extracted and presented pages 2-5 for confirmation.

message.text: This still refers to the page-2 of PDF which has the back of the book. The story begins on page-8 of the PDF, left side. Update the files and confirm again.

response.summary: Corrected PDF start offset. Re-extracted with --pdf-start-page 8 parameter. Confirmed correct page 2 content.

message.text: I checked pages 2-5 and see that you have done a good job. Now extract the rest of the pages. Create and save the story graph, write all possible stories with loop/decision restrictions. Delete cot-pages directory (bad OCR) and any superseded older scripts.

response.summary: Extracted all 111 story pages to output/cot-pages-ocr-v2/. Built story graph (58 nodes). Generated 45 story variants in output/cot-stories/. Deleted cot-pages/ and superseded scripts.

message.text: Is cot-stories-from-page-02 and cot-stories-start10 still relevant? Delete tmp page if easily regeneratable. Create SVG diagram of story graph.

response.summary: Deleted cot-stories-from-page-02, cot-stories-start10, and tmp/. Created render_story_graph_svg.py with basic SVG layout.

message.text: Look into graph layout algorithms and use one to do a better layout of the SVG graph

response.summary: Researched Sugiyama-style layered layout. Implemented topological ordering, level assignment, barycenter-based crossing reduction with 8 forward/backward iteration sweeps.

message.text: Go ahead and do: 1. color terminal pages differently, 2. highlight the main trunk from page 2

response.summary: Updated SVG renderer: terminal nodes = red (#fee2e2/#b91c1c), main trunk from page 2 = blue (#dbeafe/#1d4ed8), regular = gray (#e2e8f0/#475569). Regenerated SVG.

message.text: Write notes on this chat session for yourself into Codebase.md so next time you can read that document and continue the project without repeating things.

response.summary: Created Codebase.md as persistent project-state guide covering: canonical source dir, PDF mapping, current scripts, deleted artifacts, known OCR issues, and resume instructions.

sessions.summary: Project onboarding session — reviewed existing work, understood scope, set up session logging

message.text: This is a project i have to work on what does the instruction ask me to do [Attached: Fork-Instructions.md contents]

response.summary: Explored the full project directory. Provided a breakdown of: (1) what Fork-Instructions asked for — a web-based authoring tool for CYOA stories; (2) what was already completed in the April 8 session — OCR extraction, story graph, 45 story paths, SVG visualization; (3) what still needs to be built — the web application (authoring tool + reader).
keyFindings: Story graph: 58 nodes in cot-story-graph.mmd and cot-story-graph.svg
45 complete story variants in output/cot-stories/ with manifest.json
Codebase.md exists as project state guide
Next step: implement the web app

message.text: make a log for this conversation with this format Keep recording your AI instructions in AI-Instructions.md and chat logs (similar to AI-session-log-20260408950.json) keep it under a separate folder I want each iteration to be saved as a new log remember to log this conversation as well and the previous one

response.summary: Created session-logs/ directory. Created individual log files for the April 8 session (migrated summary) and this April 14 session.

message.text: make it into 1 json file from the session-logs folder

response.summary: Merged the two separate session log files into a single AI-session-logs.json with a top-level 'sessions' array. Deleted the two individual files.

sessions.notes: AI-Instructions.md has a rule that only the human/repo owner may edit it. The original raw Copilot log is at the project root: AI-session-log-20260408950.json

sessions.summary: Phase 1 implementation — static HTML reader with page navigation, choices, trail, and progress tracking

message.text: read all the files as well starting with the CodeBase.md

response.summary: Read all project files: Codebase.md, README.md, Fork-Instructions.md, AI-Instructions.md, all 4 scripts, cot-story-graph.mmd, manifest.json, and sample page files (02, 03, 04, 10, 46). Produced a full project overview table.

message.text: what were the suggestions for this project

response.summary: Summarized all suggestions from Fork-Instructions.md: authoring tool features (upload, branch, visualize, unfinished branches, similar endings) and reader tool. Added implied requirements from existing pipeline and a recommended build order (reader → graph viewer → authoring tool).

message.text: make a todo.md with all the task

response.summary: Created todo.md with 5 phases: Phase 1 Reader, Phase 2 Graph Viewer, Phase 3 Authoring Tool, Phase 4 Backend, Phase 5 Project Hygiene. Completed pipeline tasks checked off.

message.text: make brainstorm.md with all the ideas

response.summary: Created brainstorm.md covering reader experience (navigation, presentation, engagement), authoring tool (writing interface, graph management, structure tools, collaboration), technical architecture options, and stretch ideas.

message.text: Lets implement Phase 1

response.summary: Built the static HTML reader. Created scripts/build_pages_json.py to extract page text and choices into web/pages.json (111 pages, choices parsed from 'If you...' patterns with graph fallback). Created web/index.html with: page display, clickable choice buttons, breadcrumb trail (clickable, jump back), back/restart buttons, endings counter, smooth fade transitions, and a warm book-style design. Verified all 45 story paths navigate correctly. Served via python3 -m http.server 8765.
response.verifiedPaths: All 45 story paths navigate correctly. No pages with missing choices or broken links.

message.text: how do u run it

response.summary: Explained two-step process: (1) python3 scripts/build_pages_json.py to generate web/pages.json, (2) cd web && python3 -m http.server 8765, then open http://localhost:8765. Noted why double-clicking index.html doesn't work (browser blocks fetch() from local files).

message.text: did u add this session log json

response.summary: Confirmed the log was missing request_006 and request_007. Added both entries to session-logs/AI-session-logs.json.

message.text: there is an issue on 06-CoT.txt — incorrect OCR artifacts at top of file

response.summary: Fixed two issues in output/cot-pages-ocr-v2/06-CoT.txt: (1) removed OCR artifacts '="' and stray '6' at top of file. (2) corrected 'tun to page 22' to 'turn to page 22' — this OCR error had caused page 22 to be silently dropped from the story graph. Rebuilt story graph (now P6 --> P22 and P6 --> P114) and regenerated web/pages.json.

message.text: You are able to do multiple story lines at the same time this is incorrect. Also when the user is reading and part way they chose to go to the view story graph it continues the path they have taken. Also I would like the first page to be the author view then and full graph next to it.

response.summary: Three fixes: (1) Author View enforces single-path — picking a branch locks sibling alternatives (gray/declined, not expandable). (2) index.html saves history to localStorage when opening graph; graph.html highlights path in purple. (3) Author View is now default first tab.

message.text: when i am in the reader view sync my progress to the story graph

response.summary: Added real-time sync using localStorage + storage event. index.html now calls syncProgress() after every goTo() and goBack(), writing history to localStorage. graph.html listens for the storage event and calls updateReaderPathHighlight() to refresh the purple path highlight and pan to the current page. In Author mode, a brief flash highlights the current page if visible.

message.text: Implement Phase 2 and use this prompt: the cot-story-graph.svg represents all connections. In author mode the story selection should occur layer by layer. Author chooses a page then the scaffold expands to reveal the stories that the author can choose from (e.g. start page 2, page 3 revealed, select 3 then 4 and 5 revealed). Add to todo.md and brainstorm.md, log session.

response.summary: Built web/graph.html — interactive graph viewer using Cytoscape.js with two modes: (1) Full Graph: all 111 nodes, color-coded (blue=trunk, red=terminal, gray=regular), click any node to see page text + choices in side panel, 'Read from here' opens reader at that page. (2) Author View scaffold: layer-by-layer reveal starting from page 2, clicking a node reveals its children (amber=available, green=expanded), reset button. Updated index.html to link to graph.html and support ?page=N URL param. Updated todo.md and brainstorm.md with new scaffold concept and completed tasks.
